texto: Perhaps you still think you ought to think out beforehand and know what you ought to do. To that there is only one answer. You can only know and think about it by actually doing it. You can only learn what obedience is by obeying. It is no use asking questions
autor: for it is only through obedience that you come to learn the truth.
explicacao: Citação de for it is only through obedience that you come to learn the truth. sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Perhaps you still think you ought to think out beforehand and know what you ought to do. To that …” articula reflexao, dietrich-bonhoeffer, the-cost-of-discipleship em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como the-cost-of-discipleship.

frase_original: Perhaps you still think you ought to think out beforehand and know what you ought to do. To that there is only one answer. You can only know and think about it by actually doing it. You can only learn what obedience is by obeying. It is no use asking questions
autor_original: for it is only through obedience that you come to learn the truth.
